rulings.Case Title: WING WO MA VS. JUN TAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Moiton To Compel Corporate Party To Produce Documents At Deposition, To Extend Discovery Cutoff Date, And For Monetary Sanctions Against X-Deft
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 21, 2011, line 2. CROSS COMPLAINANTS JUN TAN AND LINDA HO'S Notice Of Hearing On Motion To Compel Corporate Party To Produce Documents At Deposition, To Extend Discovery Cutoff Date, And For Monetary Sanctions Against Cross-defendant. Defendant/cross complainants' motion to compel production of documents is granted. Corporate books and records are relevant to the alter ego allegations. Moreover, they are not protected under the attorney-client privilege or attorney-work product privilege. The California Constitution does not protect a corporation's right to privacy to its financial records. The court directs the parties to submit proposed protective orders. The insurance policy also falls under the scope of authorized discovery. The court grants a 60 day extension of the discovery cut-off date and awards sanctions in the amount of $5,000 to plaintiff's Counsel. Defendant is to remit this amount to plaintiffs counsel by July 30, 2011. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ACTING BY AND VS. STOW LAKE CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Stow Lake Corporation'S Motion To Quash Service Of Summons Or Stay Or Dismiss
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 21, 2011, line 9. DEFENDANT STOW LAKE CORPORATION'S Motion To Quash Service Of Summons Or Stay Or Dismiss. Defendant's motion to quash service is denied. Irrespective of the notice of termination being dated 2/11/10, it clearly states that the tenancy expires 3/21/11, leaving no ambiguity as to when the tenancy would terminate. This is further evidence by the defendant's application to this court in early March for a stay of that termination. Further, the temporary stay, witch dissolved upon the Court's 5/18/11 denial of defendants request for an injunction, did not invalidate the termination notice. Finally, the plaintiff rejected tender of the March and April rent payments and a portion of the February rent payment. This was consistent with its intention to terminate the tenancy. Defendants must file its answer within 5 days. =(302/LMG)
